meta.title: Getting Started - CMS Reference
meta.excerpt: Strapi setup guide and first content type creation

seo.metaDescription: Strapi setup guide and first content type creation

props.text: Step-by-step guide for setting up Strapi CMS, configuring the database, creating content types, and connecting to the Next.js frontend.

props.body:
Node.js 18.x or higher
• PostgreSQL 14+ (or SQLite for development)
• npm or yarn package manager
• Git for version control

1. Create Strapi project: npx create-strapi-app@latest my-strapi-project
• 2. Choose database (PostgreSQL recommended for production)
• 3. Configure database connection in .env
• 4. Run migrations: npm run develop
• 5. Access admin panel at http://localhost:1337/admin
• 6. Create admin user account

1. Go to Content-type Builder
• 2. Click 'Create new collection type'
• 3. Name it 'Article'
• 4. Add fields: title (String), slug (String), content (Rich Text), category (Enumeration)
• 5. Add metadata: publishedDate (Date), featured (Boolean)
• 6. Configure permissions in Settings > Roles
• 7. Save and generate API routes

REST endpoint: GET /api/articles
• GraphQL query: { articles { title slug content } }
• Automatic OpenAPI/Swagger documentation
• Type safety via generated TypeScript types

1. Install Strapi SDK: npm install @strapi/sdk
• 2. Configure API URL in next.config.js
• 3. Create API client wrapper
• 4. Use SWR or TanStack Query for data fetching
• 5. Implement server actions for mutations
• 6. Add types from generated schemas

Set STRAPI_API_URL environment variable
• Configure STRAPI_API_TOKEN for admin operations
• Set up API_WEBHOOK_SECRET for webhooks
• Enable CORS for frontend domain
• Configure rate limiting in settings
